regime_reason: The Iran ceasefire fully collapsed over the weekend (US bombed 80+ targets, Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz again) and a US naval blockade with a 20% cargo toll takes effect today 4pm EST, sending WTI +9.4% and Nasdaq -1.88%/S&P -0.78% Monday with VIX up to 17.1, and today adds June CPI plus five big-bank earnings (JPM/BAC/GS/WFC/C) as further event risk -- trend and volatility both argue against risk_on.

watchlist.note: Transocean: stock +3.65% 7/13 on continuing $1B+ Equinor harsh-environment backlog (7 rig-years, day rates >$400k) plus ~$185M Norway/Australia add; director Chad Deaton bought $173k 7/2; still pre-earnings (date TBD).

Hudson Technologies: DLA bridge-modification extended refrigerant supply contract to 11/29/26; 10%-owner Hartree Partners kept buying in the open market 7/6-7/7 at $5.99, on top of its ~$4.5M June/July cluster buy.

Absci: Phase 1 ABS-201 data (6/24) drove 8-of-9 analyst Buy ratings and PTs up to $17; stock +271% over the quarter. Caveat: priced a $100M equity offering at $7.41 same day as the data (6/24) -- real dilution alongside the real catalyst, size position accordingly.

notes: Held positions all clean today: UUUU -- CEO Bhappu bought $968k (7/7) and chairman Hansen bought (7/8), no negative news. CAI (Caris Life Sciences) -- a director bought $990k on 7/13 and the Caris Detect test launched commercially in early July; Evercore trimmed its PT to $25 from $30 (7/2) but that's still well above the ~$18 price, a soft, not hard, signal. AVO -- GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack insider buying continues, Q2's -24% revenue/avocado-price-glut miss is already priced in and called temporary by management. NTSK (Netskope) -- fresh 52-week high $21.69, Buy-rated by Wells Fargo/Jefferies/Cantor Fitzgerald this week, no negative news. Flagging again: DVLT is a live wildcard-sleeve position (18.28 sh) but sits on this desk's own avoid list (serial diluter) -- today's broker reconciliation shows it as ledger-only (broker no longer reports the position), worth a human check on whether it was actually closed out; the week's DVLT news (Mandela Dollar stablecoin deal 7/10, Dream Bowl meme-coin listing 7/6) remains speculative pump activity, not a fundamental catalyst, so the avoid call stands if it's still held anywhere.
